Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Los Angeles dbt Meetup
No reviews yet
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Alex Welch, Head of Data @dbt Labs
Topic: BI Tools & Semantic Layer
When: June 27 @ 4pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-merge channel in Slack to participate live!

Los Angeles dbt Meetup
M

Hosted by Murphy T. and Sung Won C.

Los Angeles dbt Meetup
No reviews yet
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs
🤝Venue Host: Fanduel - 6701 Center Dr W, Los Angeles, CA 90045 (www.fanduel.com/)
🍕Catering: Light bites and beverages

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda
● 5:00 - 5:25 | Check in/Registration
● 5:25 - 5:30 | Welcome Remarks by dbt Labs
● 5:30 - 6:00 | Panel featuring data leaders from Fanduel and dbt Labs
● 6:00 - 6:15 | Closing Remarks / Roadmap discussionby dbt Labs
● 6:45 - 7:00 | Networking

🗣️Presentation Details:
Presentation Description: COMING SOON!

Speaker Bio: COMING SOON!

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 4:45pm. Presentations begin at 5:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update your status if you are unable to attend so that your spot can be freed for another person. The venue has limited capacity.

DIRECTIONS:
6701 Center Dr W, Los Angeles, CA 90045 - park in the garage below the building, and head up to the FanDuel office.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-la (https://getdbt.slack.com/archives/CKHBDNHA6) channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------
dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.
Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

events.raw_text:

Mon, Mar 20, 2023 · 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM PDT

11520 W Pico Blvd

11520 W Pico Blvd · Los Angeles, CA

Los Angeles dbt Meetup [in-person]

Hosted by Sung Won C. and Census | Data A.

Los Angeles dbt Meetup
No reviews yet
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: 11520 W Pico Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90064 (https://sorrynotsorryla.com/)
🍕Catering: Light bites and drinks
🤝Organizer: dbt Labs
🤝Venue Co-Sponsor: Census & The OA Club (https://www.getcensus.com/)

Please reach out if you have any dietary restrictions or allergies.
To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda
● 5:00 - 5:25 | Check in/Registration
● 5:25 - 5:30 | Welcome Remarks by dbt Labs
● 5:30 - 6:00 | Speaker 1 + Q&A
● 6:00 - 6:30 | Speaker 2 + Q&A
● 6:30 - 6:45 | Closing Remarks by dbt Labs
● 6:45 - 7:00 | Freeform Jazz

🗣️Presentation 1 Details:
Presentation Description: Data products, like every product, need a go-to-market (GTM) strategy. Without one, your customers (stakeholders) won't understand how your products will benefit them. I'll explain why I think a GTM strategy is the solution to many of the challenges data teams face in 2023. I'll use our data team's GTM strategy at HourWork as an example.

Speaker Bio: Zack Ober is the Analytics Lead at Hourwork. He joined the data team at HourWork almost 1 year ago to build a modern data stack and develop a data culture. Zack is interested in helping people outside of the data profession understand how to use data for their benefit. He occasionally writes about this on his substack, Occam's Data.

🗣️Presentation 2 Details:
Presentation Description: When building dbt models, manual testing is still the most common way to check whether a change to your code had any unintended effects on the data. This process can slow down development and make you less likely to make changes in the future. Join us to learn how open-source data-diff can automate these tests, helping you develop quickly and confidently.

Speaker Bio: Kyle McNair is a data engineer at Datafold, and sits on the Product Experience team, with a focus on internal analytics. As a data practitioner at a data company, he partners with engineering, product, and support to help Datafold deliver data quality solutions to users.Outside of work, Kyle prefers to be outside, very far from a keyboard, responsibly imbibing UV radiation.

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 4:45pm. Presentations begin at 5:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided. Please RSVP and update status if unable to attend.

DIRECTIONS:
Sorry Not Sorry is a playful rosé-wine and cocktail-forward beer garden and restaurant located in West Los Angeles. Their 4000 square foot open air back patio is the perfect place for you to enjoy the beautiful Southern California weather. They're located right off of the 405 and within a 10 minute drive to the Santa Monica coast.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-la(https://getdbt.slack.com/archives/CKHBDNHA6) channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------
dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.
Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

The Operational Analytics Club is a Community run by data nerds, for data nerds and fostered with love by Census. Census is the #1 Data Activation platform, powered by Reverse ETL. We transform the data warehouse into a hub for marketing and business operations, empowering everyone to take action with good data. No custom scripts or engineering favors required!

Hundreds of modern companies like Fivetran, Figma, Notion, Rippling, and Canva use Census to sync their customer data to the tools that business teams rely on, achieving outcomes like personalizing marketing for over 100 million users, improving sales productivity by 10x, and reducing support times by 98%.
